<<JSON to Python Dictionary Conversion>>

SECTION 1
The provided JSON data represents an individual named John, who is 30 years old and resides in New York. To transform this structured data into a Python dictionary, we interpret the JSON keys as dictionary keys and the associated values as their corresponding entries, ensuring that the integer age remains a numeric type and the name and city remain strings.

SECTION 2
person_data = {"name": "John", "age": 30, "city": "New York"}
The resulting Python dictionary accurately reflects the original JSON structure with proper syntax and data types.
